title: 10 best herbal essential oils for acid reflux
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for acid reflux are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate the symptoms of acid reflux, a condition characterized by stomach acid flowing back up into the esophagus, causing discomfort and pain. The best herbal essential oils for acid reflux include Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Foeniculum vulgare, Cinnamomum verum, Cinchona officinalis, Mentha x piperita, Aloe barbadensis, Lavandula angustifolia, Matricaria chamomilla, and Alpinia galanga. If left untreated, acid reflux can lead to digestive issues, sleep disturbances, and even social isolation, affecting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with acid reflux because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the digestive tract. The oil's active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and licoricidin, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, allowing food and stomach acid to move through the digestive system more easily. Additionally, herbal licorice essential oils can help relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and reducing symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can contribute to acid reflux and alleviate symptoms like heartburn and chest pain.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in licorice essential oil neutralize free radicals that can damage the stomach lining and worsen acid reflux, helping to protect the stomach from oxidative stress.

Soothing and mucilaginous properties: Licorice essential oil's mucilages coat and soothe the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by acid reflux and providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain compounds like glycyrrhizin and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate acid reflux symptoms.

Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, making them effective in soothing digestive issues associated with acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered roots with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a dark glass bottle.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a cool place away from direct sunlight, shaking the bottle daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean dark glass bottle discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Store the resulting essential oil in an amber-colored glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, keeping it away from light and heat for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with acid reflux because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining. It also has a soothing effect on the digestive system, easing the passage of food through the body and reducing symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and bloating. Additionally, ginger's natural antibacterial properties can help eliminate harmful bacteria that may contribute to acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can exacerbate acid reflux symptoms.
Antacid: The oils' antacid properties help neutralize stomach acid, providing relief from heartburn and other acid reflux symptoms.
Digestive aid: Ginger essential oil has natural digestive properties that stimulate digestion and relieve nausea, bloating, and discomfort associated with acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is the underground stem that stores nutrients and has anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in soothing and calming the digestive system to alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
Roots: The roots are a rich source of antioxidants and have natural anti-acidic properties that can help neutralize stomach acids and reduce inflammation in the esophagus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes for extraction.
Steam distillation process begins by adding 500 milliliters water.
Essential oil collected as condensate in a separating funnel.
Transfer essential oil to a dark glass bottle for storage.
Allow essential oil to mature for 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with acid reflux because of its natural antacid properties, which help to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. The oil's active compounds, such as anethole, have been shown to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, fennel essential oils' anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing discomfort and pain associated with acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Fennel essential oil has a carminative effect, which means it helps to expel gas from the digestive system, reducing discomfort and symptoms associated with acid reflux.
Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of fennel essential oil help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and contractions that can exacerbate acid reflux.

Digestive stimulant property: Fennel essential oil has a stimulating effect on digestion, which helps to improve the movement of food through the digestive system, reducing the likelihood of stomach contents flowing back up into the esophagus and causing acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are known for their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues like acid reflux.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a compound called anethole, which has natural antacid properties that can help neutralize stomach acid and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare seeds in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal oil production.
Dry the harvested seeds thoroughly in a single layer for 7-10 days or until they reach 10% moisture content.
Steam distill the dried seeds using a still with a condenser at 100°c for 2-3 hours to extract essential oils and sesquiterpenes.
Filter the resulting distillate through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove any impurities or sediment.
Transfer the filtered essential oil into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, labeling them with date of production and storage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with acid reflux because of its unique properties that soothe and calm the digestive system. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the esophagus, while its anxiolytic effects help alleviate stress-induced stomach acid production. Additionally, chamomile's natural antacid properties help neutralize excess stomach acid, providing rapid relief from heartburn and discomfort. This natural remedy promotes a sense of relaxation and digestive harmony, making it an effective solution for managing acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile essential oil reduces inflammation in the esophagus, which can alleviate symptoms of acid reflux by minimizing damage to the esophageal lining.
Antispasmodic: Chamomile essential oil helps relax the muscles in the lower esophageal sphincter (LES), preventing spasms that can cause acid reflux.
Anxiolytic: Chamomile essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety-related factors that can contribute to acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant are rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing and protecting the digestive tract against acid reflux.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain apigenin, an antioxidant flavonoid that has a calming effect on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them thoroughly to remove excess moisture.
Combine dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with carrier oil in a ratio of 1:3 and let it steep for 14 days.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container to separate the essential oil from solids.
Place the filtered liquid under low heat and gentle stirring for 2 hours to enhance volatile compounds extraction.
Allow the resulting matricaria chamomilla essential oil to cool and settle before bottling and storing in a dark glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon essential oils helps with acid reflux because of its natural antacid properties. The oil's active compound, cinnamaldehyde, has been shown to stimulate digestive enzymes and relieve stomach inflammation, which can help reduce the occurrence of acid reflux symptoms such as heartburn and bloating. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's ability to relax the muscles in the esophagus and stomach can also help alleviate discomfort and promote a sense of relief for individuals suffering from acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil's antioxidant property helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory property of Ceylon cinnamon essential oil reduces inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can help to soothe and calm heartburn and acid reflux symptoms.

Carminative property: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil's carminative property helps to reduce bloating and gas by relaxing muscles in the digestive tract, reducing pressure on the LES (lower esophageal sphincter), and preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems that store nutrients, are rich in bioactive compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which helps alleviate symptoms of acid reflux by reducing inflammation and relaxant properties.

Barks: The barks of Ceylon cinnamon contain a high concentration of cinnamic acid, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in soothing acid reflux and gut issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cinnamomum verum bark at dawn or dusk to ensure optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested bark in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Steam distill the powder using a clevenger apparatus with a ratio of 1 part powder to 2 parts water for 3 hours.
Filter and decant the resulting essential oil, discarding any sediment and storing it in dark glass bottles for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime essential oils helps with acid reflux because its antispasmodic properties help to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, allowing stomach acid to flow back up and alleviate symptoms. Additionally, its natural anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can irritate and exacerbate acid reflux symptoms. By soothing and calming these areas, broad-leaved lime essential oils provide relief from heartburn and indigestion discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antacid and anti-inflammatory properties: Lime essential oil has been shown to neutralize acidity in the stomach and reduce inflammation in the esophageal mucosa, which can help alleviate heartburn and discomfort associated with acid reflux.

Digestive enzyme stimulant: The citral and limonene present in lime essential oil have been found to stimulate digestive enzymes, improving digestion and reducing symptoms of acid reflux such as bloating and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Lime essential oil contains antioxidants that help protect the esophageal lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the broad-leaved lime plant are used to create essential oils that help soothe and calm stomach issues, including acid reflux due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds.

Barks: The barks of the broad-leaved lime plant contain bioflavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia platyphyllos leaves and flowers in the early morning, ensuring an adequate quantity for processing.
Dry the harvested material using a dehydrator or air circulation method for 12 hours at 35°c.
Steam distill the dried plant material with water in a steam distillation apparatus for 4-6 hours, collecting essential oil and condensate.
Separate the collected essential oil from the condensate using a separation funnel, filtering it through cotton wool or paper to remove impurities.
Store the resulting tilia platyphyllos essential oil in dark glass bottles, sealing tightly and labeling accurately for future use and shelf life up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with acid reflux because it has a soothing and calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn and GERD. The oil's natural antacid properties help to neutralize excess stomach acid, providing relief from burning sensations and discomfort in the chest and throat. Additionally, lemon balm's carminative properties help to ease digestion, preventing the upward movement of stomach acid into the esophagus and alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm essential oil helps to relax the smooth muscle in the esophagus and reduce spasms, which can ease the passage of stomach contents and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the esophageal lining, which can further exacerbate acid reflux symptoms, by reducing swelling and pain.

Antioxidant property: Lemon balm essential oil's antioxidant properties help to protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to acid reflux and other digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain high amounts of citral, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers, collecting approximately 20 pounds for processing.
Dry the collected plant material using a food dehydrator set at 100°f for 12 hours or until crumbly.
Grind the dried plant material into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Steam-distill the ground plant material in a stainless steel still at atmospheric pressure, collecting 1 liter of essential oil over 4 hours.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or fine-mesh strainer to remove any remaining impurities and sediment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop essential oils helps with acid reflux because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the digestive system. The oil's antimicrobial agents also work to reduce the growth of bacteria in the stomach, thereby decreasing the production of excessive acid that can contribute to acid reflux symptoms. Additionally, hyssop oil has a calming effect on the esophageal muscles, allowing them to function properly and preventing the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can alleviate symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and discomfort.

Antacid properties: The oil contains antacid compounds that can neutralize excess stomach acid and reduce its corrosive effects on the esophageal lining, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms like regurgitation and chest pain.

Soothing and calming properties: Hyssop essential oil has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress-related symptoms that may trigger or worsen acid reflux, promoting a sense of relaxation and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of hyssop are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective at soothing digestive issues like acid reflux.
Seeds: Hyssop seeds contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have natural antacid properties that can help neutralize stomach acids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality hyssopus officinalis leaves and flowers for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 30°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material in a clevenger apparatus with distilled water for 4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the water using a separating funnel and filter paper.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid at room temperature for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder essential oils helps with acid reflux because of its unique properties that soothe and calm the digestive tract. remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers in mid-summer when they are fully bloomed, ensuring at least 100g of fresh material.
Dry the harvested flowers using a low temperature oven or food dehydrator set at 30°c for 12 hours, resulting in crisp and brittle texture.
Transfer dried flowers to a clean glass jar and cover with 250ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, allowing it to steep for 14 days.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding solids and retaining the liquid extract for further processing.
Distill the extracted liquid using a steam distillation apparatus at atmospheric pressure for 1 hour, collecting the resulting essential oil in a clean glass vial.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with acid reflux because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the digestive tract. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus, allowing food to pass more smoothly through the digestive system and preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers at peak bloom and dry them immediately using a clean cloth or paper towels to absorb excess moisture for 24 hours.
Crush dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes until they reach the desired consistency.
Mix crushed calendula with high-quality carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a 1:4 ratio by weight for a total of 250 milliliters.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 14 days, shaking occasionally, before filtering it using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Transfer filtered liquid to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in a cool, dry place for up to six months or until the essential oil has separated and formed a distinct layer.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat acid reflux with herbal essential oils is by using a blend of calming and anti-inflammatory oils. Combine equal parts of ginger oil, peppermint oil, and lemon balm oil in a carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil. Apply topically to the chest area before meals to soothe the esophagus and reduce inflammation. Additionally, inhale the vapors from a diffuser or take a few drops orally with water to further alleviate symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acid reflux that are treated with herbal essential oils are digestive issues such as bloating, gas, and indigestion. Certain essential oils like peppermint, ginger, and fennel have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of discomfort. These oils can be used in aromatherapy, massage, or added to herbal teas for relief from these gastrointestinal complaints.
